complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Register Canvas Commands in Command Palette
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Create canvasCommands.tsx with comprehensive canvas operations: 1) Block addition commands - integrate with canvasStore to add blocks at default/center position, 2) View commands (zoom, fit, grid toggle) - these need to call canvasStore actions or dispatch viewport changes, 3) Edit commands (delete, duplicate, select all, alignment) - leverage existing canvasStore selection and component operations, 4) Export commands (PNG, BOM, print) - integrate with existing export utilities in OneCanvas/utils. complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Higher complexity due to the large number of commands (~20+), multiple command categories (add, view, edit, export), and need to integrate with canvasStore which has complex selection and component management. Some features like alignment/distribution may need new store actions or utilities. Export commands need to integrate with existing bomGenerator.ts, canvasExport.ts, and printSupport.ts utilities. The canvasStore is 100+ lines with Maps and complex state.

Risk of breaking existing undo/redo if not careful. Testing is critical to ensure history still works correctly.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Enhance existing ScopeExpandedView.tsx (321 lines) with additional features: 1) Add Force Trigger button alongside existing Run/Stop, calling scope_arm_trigger command, 2) Add trigger level input (numeric) and trigger edge toggle (Rising/Falling) to trigger controls section, 3) Improve measurements display with Vmin, Vmax, RMS calculations, 4) Consider adding timeline/seek functionality for paused waveform review. Enhancements needed: force trigger button, trigger level/edge controls, improved measurements. Some features like timeline scrubbing would require new state and canvas interaction. complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Create scope-simulation integration in the Rust backend: 1) Identify where simulation loop runs (likely in src-tauri/src/sim/ directory) and understand node voltage calculation flow, 2) Create route_scope_inputs function to find scope blocks, trace connected wires to voltage sources, 3) Add scope sampling calls during simulation tick, ensuring proper sample rate synchronization, 4) Handle disconnected/disabled channels gracefully, 5) Consider voltage scaling and channel scale settings. This requires deep understanding of both the simulation engine and scope engine architecture.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Higher complexity due to cross-system integration. Requires understanding the simulation architecture (canvas_sync.rs, sim module), how node voltages are calculated, the scope engine's sampling mechanism, and timing synchronization. The ScopeBlock.tsx already calls scope_create and polls scope_get_data, but actual voltage values need to flow from simulation. This is a core infrastructure task affecting multiple Rust modules.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add Debug Commands to Command Palette
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Create debugCommands.tsx following established command patterns: 1) Define debug state types and determine where debug state lives (possibly new debugStore or extension to simulationService), 2) Implement breakpoint commands - need to define what 'breakpoint' means in ladder/canvas context and how to persist them, 3) Implement execution control commands that work with simulation pause/step functionality, 4) Add watch list infrastructure if not existing. complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate complexity. The command infrastructure is well-established. However, the debugging feature itself may need more foundational work: where breakpoints are stored, how they integrate with the simulation/ladder elements, what 'step into/out' means for PLC logic. Some commands (continue, pause) may overlap with existing simulation commands. The scope is somewhat open-ended regarding the actual debugging infrastructure.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add Scope Cursor and Measurement Features
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Add cursor measurement system to ScopeExpandedView: 1) Define CursorState interface and add to component state (enabled, positions, active cursor), 2) Implement cursor rendering on canvas - add to waveformRenderer.ts with dashed lines, labels, and position markers, 3) Add cursor interaction handlers (click to place, drag to move, keyboard fine-tuning), 4) Create measurement display panel showing ΔT, ΔV, frequency, individual cursor values, 5) Add auto-measurement calculations (frequency detection from zero-crossings, rise/fall time, duty cycle, RMS). Consider making measurements persistent in scope settings.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate-high complexity. Requires canvas interaction for cursor placement/dragging, mathematical calculations for measurements, UI for cursor controls and measurement display. The waveformRenderer.ts exists but needs cursor overlay logic. Auto-measurements require waveform analysis algorithms (zero-crossing detection, RMS calculation). This is a feature-complete cursor system similar to real oscilloscopes, which is non-trivial.
